text: The clutch exercise is one of the simplest and easiest exercises to complete and get a high score on on the simulator. When one of the green globes lights up, you will be required to touch that globe with the correct hand.

The simulator displays green globes at varying distances. The surgeon uses the clutch function to extend the reach of the instrument to touch the furthest globes, then clutches in the opposite direction to return and touch the closest globes.
Using your camera will actually disqualify you from this exercise. Once you feel comfortable using the clutch mechanism on the hand controls, this exercise is easy to master.
